key_insight: Continual motion-language agents can preserve both motion-to-text and text-to-motion performance across sequential tasks by using LoRA-based low-rank adaptation with expert routing to reduce catastrophic forgetting.
engineering_relevance: This work is relevant to adaptive embodied AI systems because it provides a practical pathway for lifelong learning of new motion concepts without retraining a full foundation model.
method: Frozen large language model backbone with LoRA variants for continual adaptation
Mixture-of-experts design with an autoencoder-based router for task selection at inference
Five-task benchmark built from HumanML3D via semantic clustering of motion descriptions
evidence_signal: The authors evaluate on a reproducible five-task sequential benchmark and report near-zero forgetting while maintaining strong captioning and generation quality in both M2T and T2M settings.
watch_item: Hard routing improves quality, but the approach depends on reliable expert selection and may be sensitive to router errors or mismatches between token-level accuracy and true generation quality.
paper_title: Towards Continual Motion-Language Agents: LoRA Variants for Incremental Motion Understanding and Generation
